title: feat: add app URL tracking and BYOP settings for publishable keys
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: Name tags for your apps
gist.blurb: Apps can now introduce themselves properly. We've added app attribution to the authorization screen and new Bring Your Own Pollen settings for developers.
gist.summary: Developers can now attach app URLs and Bring Your Own Pollen (BYOP) settings to publishable keys, enabling richer attribution on the user authorization screen.
gist.impact: Users will now see the specific app name and developer GitHub handle when authorizing apps, making it clearer who they are sharing their pollen with.
gist.visual_concept: The pollinations bee acting as a friendly greeter, checking a cute pixelated ID badge or name tag held by a robot. The ID badge represents the new app attribution and BYOP settings.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny greeter's visor, inspecting a glowing digital name tag held by a chunky monitor-head robot. The name tag shows a tiny pixelated app icon. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
